_comment: ccc-agent runtime config. Install (root-owned, world-readable) at /etc/ccc-agent/config.json or /opt/ccc-agent/config/config.json. The agent must never be able to write this file or anything under state_dir. In CCC images this file is generated at container startup by ccc-agent-setup (it needs runtime-only paths: the container name + /storage layout), NOT at image build.
_state_comment: Holds the BranchFS mountpoints, so it MUST be under a CCC bind mount (the FUSE sidecar refuses the container overlay like /opt). /storage/user always is; it is hidden from the branch via hide_paths. For node-local speed use a bind-mounted local scratch, e.g. /storage/local/ssd/.ccc-agent.
_alias_comment: home_subdir feeds the policy alias /home/<user> -> /storage/user/<home_subdir>. CCC mounts the user home to the same bytes as /storage/user/<container>, so set this to the container name to dedup them in policy.
_confinement_comment: bwrap = real rootless sandbox (default). none = debug only, NOT a boundary.
_runtime_comment: The agent binary + deps live under the real $HOME / storage that the view hides; re-expose them read-only here. Prefer dests OUTSIDE the views (e.g. install agents at /opt) to avoid bwrap mkdir'ing mountpoints into the FUSE view. 'src' or 'src:dest'.
_cred_comment: Mount the agent's config/state dirs read-only from the real home so it finds them. OAuth-subscription logins authenticate from the token FILE, which must stay readable (do NOT mask it); cred_mask + cred_env are only for API-key auth (mask the file, pass the key by env).
_roots_comment: ONE BranchFS root over the whole of /storage: base is the real read-through underlay, store is a node-local delta dir OUTSIDE /storage, visible is where the branch is mounted back inside the sandbox. home_subdir (relative to the branch) is bind-mounted to /home/<user>, so the home and /storage/user/<container> share a single branch instead of being overlaid twice.

policy._policy_comment: workspace-auto: changes under the workspace (the home) auto-commit; anything else under /storage (datasets, group, other users) is read-through but flagged for review on write. Agent config/cache churn is ignored.
